DECLARATION OF KEITH KAWAMURA IN SUPPORT OF RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S OPPOS TO REQUEST FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION FILED BY RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND ITS PLANNING DEPARTMENT

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER THE UPPER NOE ENVIRONMENTAL COALITION'S HEARING ON PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. IT APPEARS UNLIKELY THAT PETITIONER WILL PREVAIL ON THE MERITS. THE CLAIM APPEARS TO BE BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS AND THAT THE APPROVED DOG RUN DOES NOT REQUIRE C.E.Q.A. REVIEW. PETITIONER HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE IRREPARABLE HARM DUE TO INCREASED NOISE AND/OR HARM TO THE ENVIRONMENT. MOREOVER IF PETITIONER WERE ULTIMATELY TO PREVAIL, THE CITY COULD BE ORDERED TO REMOVE THE DOG RUN. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUN-12-2007 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

Off Calendar Jun-12-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER THE UPPER NOE ENVIRONMENTAL COALITION'S HEARING ON PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION.
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. IT APPEARS UNLIKELY THAT PETITIONER WILL PREVAIL ON THE MERITS. THE CLAIM APPEARS TO BE BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS AND THAT THE APPROVED DOG RUN DOES NOT REQUIRE C.E.Q.A. REVIEW. PETITIONER HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE IRREPARABLE HARM DUE TO INCREASED NOISE AND/OR HARM TO THE ENVIRONMENT. MOREOVER IF PETITIONER WERE ULTIMATELY TO PREVAIL, THE CITY COULD BE ORDERED TO REMOVE THE DOG RUN. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600 (302/PJM/AP)
